title: feat(mcp): add suggest_issues_from_findings tool for post-triage issue creation
body:
## Summary

Add a new MCP tool that analyzes triaged findings and recommends which ones should become GitHub issues. This helps coding assistants (regardless of skill support) identify valuable follow-up work from code review feedback.

## Problem

After triaging review findings, some acknowledged items warrant tracking as GitHub issues while others don't. Currently:
1. Coding assistants must manually decide which findings deserve issues
2. Related findings aren't grouped (3 performance findings → should be 1 issue)
3. No heuristics help distinguish "nice to have" from "worth tracking"
4. Skills can provide guidance, but not all assistants support skills

## Proposed Solution

### New Tool: `suggest_issues_from_findings`

```go
type SuggestIssuesInput struct {
    Owner      string   `json:"owner"`
    Repo       string   `json:"repo"`
    PRNumber   int      `json:"pr_number"`
    // If empty, analyzes all acknowledged/wont_fix findings
    FindingIDs []string `json:"finding_ids,omitempty"`
    // Minimum confidence threshold (0.0-1.0) for suggestions
    MinConfidence float64 `json:"min_confidence,omitempty"` // default: 0.5
}

type SuggestIssuesOutput struct {
    Suggestions []IssueSuggestion `json:"suggestions"`
    Skipped     int               `json:"skipped"`      // Findings not worth issues
    Message     string            `json:"message"`
}

type IssueSuggestion struct {
    FindingIDs     []string `json:"finding_ids"`      // Grouped related findings
    SuggestedTitle string   `json:"suggested_title"`
    SuggestedBody  string   `json:"suggested_body"`   // Draft issue body
    IssueType      string   `json:"issue_type"`       // enhancement, bug, tech-debt, security
    Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence"`       // 0.0-1.0
    Rationale      string   `json:"rationale"`        // Why this deserves tracking
}
```

### Heuristics for Issue-Worthiness

| Factor | Weight | Rationale |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| Category = security | +0.3 | Security issues should be tracked |
| Category = architecture | +0.2 | Architectural debt worth tracking |
| Category = performance | +0.2 | Performance issues often need dedicated work |
| Category = bug | +0.2 | Bugs should be fixed |
| Category = style/maintainability | -0.1 | Often too minor |
| Status = acknowledged | +0.2 | Explicitly deferred for later |
| Status = wont_fix | -0.3 | Intentionally not addressing |
| Has concrete suggestion | +0.2 | Actionable = more valuable |
| Multiple related findings | +0.2 | Pattern = systematic fix needed |
| Severity = high/critical | +0.2 | Higher severity = more important |
| Severity = low | -0.1 | Lower priority |

Base confidence starts at 0.4, factors adjust up/down, capped at 0.0-1.0.

### Grouping Related Findings

Findings are grouped into a single issue suggestion when:
- Same category (e.g., all "performance" findings)
- Similar description patterns (fuzzy match)
- Same file or related files
- Explicitly linked via suggestion text

Example:
```json
{
  "finding_ids": ["abc123", "def456", "ghi789"],
  "suggested_title": "perf: optimize file handling in review_files tool",
  "issue_type": "enhancement",
  "confidence": 0.75,
  "rationale": "3 related performance findings in review_handlers.go suggest systematic optimization opportunity"
}
```

### Enhancement: Issue-Worthy Hints in Finding Output

Additionally, enhance `FindingOutput` to include early signals:

```go
type FindingOutput struct {
    // ...existing...
    IssueWorthy     *bool   `json:"issue_worthy,omitempty"`
    IssueWorthyHint *string `json:"issue_worthy_hint,omitempty"`
}
```

These hints are computed when findings are returned from `list_findings`, `review_pr`, etc., giving assistants early visibility before triage completes.

## Usage Example

After triage:
```json
// Input
{
  "owner": "bkyoung",
  "repo": "code-reviewer",
  "pr_number": 211
}

// Output
{
  "suggestions": [
    {
      "finding_ids": ["e24e2eca...", "64c0fb75..."],
      "suggested_title": "perf(mcp): optimize file I/O in review_files tool",
      "suggested_body": "## Summary\n\nMultiple performance findings from PR #211 suggest optimization opportunities...\n\n## Findings\n- Read file header before full content\n- Avoid double file open\n\n## Origin\nPR #211 code review",
      "issue_type": "enhancement",
      "confidence": 0.72,
      "rationale": "2 related performance findings with concrete suggestions"
    },
    {
      "finding_ids": ["bf319260..."],
      "suggested_title": "feat(mcp): track skipped files count in review_files output",
      "suggested_body": "...",
      "issue_type": "enhancement",
      "confidence": 0.65,
      "rationale": "Architecture finding with clear user-facing improvement"
    }
  ],
  "skipped": 3,
  "message": "Analyzed 5 acknowledged findings: 2 issue suggestions, 3 not worth tracking"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Implement `suggest_issues_from_findings` tool
- [ ] Heuristic scoring for issue-worthiness
- [ ] Grouping of related findings into single suggestions
- [ ] Generate draft issue title and body
- [ ] Configurable confidence threshold
- [ ] Add `issue_worthy` hints to `FindingOutput`
- [ ] Tests for heuristics and grouping logic
- [ ] Documentation

## Future Enhancements

- Learn from user feedback (which suggestions were accepted/rejected)
- Integration with `batch_reply_to_findings` to surface suggestions inline
- Option to auto-create issues above a confidence threshold
- Link created issues back to original findings
